key_insight: Two-dimensional quasicrystals can store and retrieve cyclic-shear memory through hierarchical, nested hysteresis loops generated by reversible phason-like tile-switching events.
engineering_relevance: This identifies a microscopic bistable mechanism for mechanical memory in a nonperiodic solid, informing constitutive models for cyclic loading and adaptive metamaterials.
method: Athermal quasistatic shear simulations on 2D dodecagonal quasicrystals
Cyclic shear with varying amplitudes to probe loop-return point memory
Resolution of local reversible plastic events and comparison with a periodic approximant
evidence_signal: The abstract reports persistent shear bands above yielding, nested hysteresis loops below yielding, and localized tile-switch hysterons as the recovered memory units.
watch_item: The memory mechanism is demonstrated in idealized simulations, so translation to real materials will depend on whether phason dynamics and bistable rearrangements remain accessible experimentally.
paper_title: How Quasicrystals Remember: Hierarchical Memory Under Cyclic Shear
